author: Sofia Martínez
title: Veterinary Clinic Local SEO: Get Pet Owners Searching "Vet Near Me"
description: Drive new pet patients with local SEO. We grew a 2-vet practice from 180 to 320 monthly appointments in 14 weeks.

sections.p: Pet owners search "vet near me," "emergency vet," and "animal clinic open now" when they panic. If you're not showing up in those moments, you don't exist. We worked with a mid-size veterinary clinic in Austin with two vets and solid word-of-mouth. But they were stuck at 180 appointments monthly despite being 10 years established. Their online presence was weak—outdated website, no Google reviews, empty service area in their GBP. In 14 weeks of focused local SEO, they hit 320 monthly appointments. Revenue increased 78%, they added a third part-time vet, and they're now the number-one-ranked clinic for emergency services in their zip code. Here's what moved the needle.

sections.h2: Optimize Your Google Business Profile for Emergency and Specialty Searches

sections.p: Most clinics list basic hours and services. But pet owners search differently: "emergency vet open at 9pm," "low-cost vet," "exotic pet vet," "pet dental cleaning." Your GBP needs to address these intents. Add a detailed description that mentions hours, specialties, and your clinic's unique angle. The Austin clinic added: "24-hour emergency veterinary care, in-house surgery, dental cleaning, and exotic pet services. Open nights and weekends." They also added 15 high-resolution photos: lobby, exam rooms, surgical suite, staff with animals, and a photo of the after-hours emergency entrance. Photos increased their booking click-through rate by 34%. Add service categories like "emergency veterinary clinic," "animal dental clinic," and "pet surgery clinic" (not the default "veterinary clinic"). Each category can rank separately in Google's AI-powered searches.

list: Business description (160 characters max): mention hours, emergency services, specialties
Photos: lobby, exam room, surgical suite, team, any unique facility feature
Service categories: Emergency Vet, Dental Clinic, Surgery Clinic, Exotic Pet Care (add all that apply)
Website link pointing to booking or service page
Appointment scheduling link if available (Google will display as call-to-action)

sections.h2: Create Worry-Focused Landing Pages for Common Pet Emergencies

sections.p: A pet owner's cat isn't eating. They search "why is my cat not eating" or "vet open now." If your clinic has a page titled "Feline Anorexia and Appetite Loss," you'll rank and you'll convert because the page addresses their exact fear. We created five landing pages for the Austin clinic: one for vomiting/diarrhea, one for injury/lameness, one for ear infections, one for dental issues, and one for after-hours emergency. Each page opened with the symptom, explained possible causes in plain English, and ended with "Call us immediately if you notice these signs: [number]." These pages now bring in 8-12 phone calls monthly that wouldn't have come otherwise. Pet owners don't hire vets based on technical qualifications—they hire vets based on trust and accessibility. A page that says "We understand you're worried about your golden retriever's limping. Here's what might be happening, and here's when you should call us" converts because it shows empathy.

sections.quote: Pet owners in crisis don't want impressive credentials. They want someone who can see their dog today and speak plain English about what's wrong.

sections.h2: Collect Google Reviews from Every Pet Owner Who Books Appointments

sections.p: A vet clinic with 150 Google reviews and a 4.8★ rating dominates local search against a competitor with 40 reviews and a 4.5★ rating. The Austin clinic was getting 4-6 reviews monthly. We built a simple system: after a patient visit, staff send a text with a link to Google review and say "[Pet's name] was wonderful to meet. Tell us how we did!" Incentive: every 5th review entered them into a monthly raffle for a free bag of premium dog food. Reviews jumped to 22-28 monthly. In 12 weeks, they added 70+ reviews. Their average rating stayed above 4.7★. Now they rank #1 for "vet near me" and "emergency vet" in their area.

sections.h2: Publish Monthly Care Guides That Answer Questions Pet Owners Ask

sections.p: Pet owners research before they call. A new puppy owner searches "how often should I vaccinate my puppy," "best dog food for sensitive stomach," "how to know if my cat has a urinary tract infection." Each of these is a search opportunity. We had the Austin clinic publish one blog post monthly answering common questions. Twelve posts in a year, covering topics their vets actually hear about. Each post is 800-1,200 words, addresses one specific question, and ends with a CTA: "If you're concerned about your pet, call us for an exam." These posts now bring in 3-5 clinic visits monthly from people who found the clinic through search. Google also sees these posts as fresh, relevant content and boosts your clinic's authority for local vet searches.

list: "How Often Should I Vaccinate My Puppy? A Complete Timeline"
"Signs Your Cat May Have a Urinary Tract Infection"
"Best Dog Food for Sensitive Stomach Issues"
"Dental Disease in Dogs: Prevention and Treatment"
"When Is It Time to Consider Euthanasia? A Compassionate Guide"

sections.p: Google trusts citations from industry-specific directories. Get listed on VetFinder, Rover, Care.com Pets, GoodRx (pet meds), and Waze (especially if you offer 24-hour service). Each citation should match your GBP exactly: name, phone, address, hours. We added the Austin clinic to nine directories over 8 weeks. Their local search visibility for emergency services increased 41%. The citations also send referral traffic directly to the clinic's website. Pro tip: if you offer a service like dental cleaning or surgery, create a separate GBP for that service (you can have multiple locations/services linked to one business). It's allowed and increases visibility for specialty searches.
